project_meta.name: Gerpain ERP
project_meta.description: Bakery chain ERP — core loop: Deliveries → Cash Collections → Dashboard. Currency: XOF/FCFA. Core loop complete. Next: performance, dashboard, UX polish. POS deferred. See docs/EXPECTATIONS.md.

backlog.description: Admin signup/login, token-based API auth, organization auto-created on first login.
acceptance_criteria: Auth works end-to-end
Organization created on first login

backlog.feature: Bakery schema, API, selector, and management page
backlog.description: Bakeries table, CRUD API with tier limits, BakerySelector in sidebar, bakeries management page, API clients send X-Bakery-ID header.
acceptance_criteria: Bakeries CRUD works
BakerySelector switches context
API clients scoped to bakery

backlog.description: Locations (shop/warehouse) CRUD under selected bakery. Backend filters by X-Bakery-ID.
acceptance_criteria: Locations CRUD works within bakery context

backlog.description: Employees list wired to API with create/edit/activate/deactivate. Roles: delivery, cashier, manager, baker.
acceptance_criteria: Employee CRUD works
Loading/error/empty states

backlog.feature: Product categories, products catalog, pricing rules
backlog.description: Categories CRUD, products CRUD with category filtering, location-specific pricing overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Categories CRUD
Products CRUD with price in FCFA
Pricing rules per location

backlog.description: Inventory items per product per location with status indicators, adjustments, and filtering.
acceptance_criteria: Stock levels display
Adjustments work
Low stock indicators

backlog.description: View/edit organization name, description, address, currency. Slug read-only.
acceptance_criteria: Settings page works

backlog.feature: Employee product assignments and per-product commission
backlog.description: Add employee_products junction table: each delivery/cashier employee gets assigned products with a per-product commission rate (FCFA/unit). This drives which products appear in their daily delivery form and how commission is computed. Add baseSalary field to employees. Update employee create/edit UI with product assignment panel (checkbox + commission input per product). See EXPECTATIONS.md #15.
acceptance_criteria: Create employee_products table: employeeId, productId, commissionPerUnit (integer FCFA), isActive
Add baseSalary (integer FCFA) to employees table
Generate and apply Drizzle migration
Backend: GET /employees/:id/products, PUT /employees/:id/products (bulk upsert)
Frontend: employee create/edit drawer shows product assignment panel with checkboxes + commission input
Products list comes from useProducts() for the bakery
Saving employee also saves product assignments
verification: Create employee with product assignments and verify in DB

backlog.feature: Wire deliveries page to real API (pre-populated daily form)
backlog.description: Rewrite /sales/deliveries data layer. Keep existing UI layout but swap mock data for real API. Backend auto-creates draft delivery runs for all active delivery employees when a date is first accessed (GET /delivery-runs?date=YYYY-MM-DD). Each run pre-populated with the employee's assigned products (fallback: all active bakery products) at qty 0. Frontend: replace mockEmployees/mockProducts with useEmployees/useProducts hooks. Add ◀/▶ date navigation + calendar. Wire save (PATCH) and validate (POST /runs/:id/validate) to API. Revenue = sold × unitPrice in FCFA. See EXPECTATIONS.md #16-24.
acceptance_criteria: GET /delivery-runs?date= returns runs for all active delivery employees (auto-creates drafts if none exist)
Each draft run has deliveryItems for the employee's assigned products at qty 0
Frontend loads real employees and products from API (no mock data)
◀/▶ date navigation buttons + calendar picker
Summary table shows all agents with totals (even unfilled drafts at 0)
Detail view shows all products with Matin/Soir quantity inputs
Save persists quantities to DB via PATCH
Validate locks the run (status=validated, validatedAt set)
Revenue calculations: sold = entrusted - returned, revenue = sold × unitPrice
Validated runs are read-only in UI
verification: Open deliveries page, see all agents listed, fill quantities, save, validate

backlog.feature: Schema patch — add deliveryRunId, isSettled, period to cashCollections
backlog.description: Add three fields to cashCollections table: deliveryRunId (FK to deliveryRuns, nullable — links collection to source delivery), isSettled (boolean, default false — payroll settlement flag), period (text, nullable — flexible payroll period label like 'Jan-2026', 'Week-3', '01/02-15/02'). Update relations and Zod schema. Generate and apply Drizzle migration. See EXPECTATIONS.md Schema Gaps.
acceptance_criteria: cashCollections.deliveryRunId FK added (nullable, references deliveryRuns.id)
cashCollections.isSettled boolean added (default false)
cashCollections.period text added (nullable)
cashCollectionsRelations updated with deliveryRun relation
deliveryRunsRelations updated with cashCollection relation
insertCashCollectionSchema updated
Drizzle migration generated and applied

backlog.feature: Auto-create cash collection on delivery validation
backlog.description: When a delivery run is validated (POST /runs/:id/validate), the backend automatically creates a cashCollection record with: expectedAmount = sum of (sold × unitPrice) for all items in the run, employeeId/locationId/date from the run, deliveryRunId = run.id, status = 'pending'. If delivery is re-validated after correction, update the existing collection's expectedAmount. See EXPECTATIONS.md #31-33, #46-48.
acceptance_criteria: Validating a delivery run creates a cashCollection with correct expectedAmount
Collection linked to delivery via deliveryRunId FK
expectedAmount = sum of ((quantityEntrusted - quantityReturned) × unitPrice) for all items
If collection already exists for this run, update expectedAmount instead of creating duplicate
Frontend shows toast: 'Tournée validée — collecte de X FCFA créée automatiquement' with link to collections
verification: Validate a delivery, check collection appears with correct amount in DB

backlog.feature: Cash collection recording — employee-centric period view
backlog.description: Rewrite /cash/collections as an employee-centric period view. Top bar: employee selector (with role badge: Livreur/Caissier) + period selector (Cette semaine, Ce mois, Derniers 15 jours, Personnalisée). Summary cards: total expected, total collected, outstanding balance, collection rate %. Collections table: one row per day for selected employee in period, showing date, source (Livraison/Boutique), expected, collected, écart, status, actions. Inline expand for recording payment (cash + card + mobile breakdown). Delivery detail summary in each row. Validate/reject workflow. 'Marquer la période comme réglée' button sets isSettled=true. See EXPECTATIONS.md #34-42.
acceptance_criteria: Employee selector shows all delivery agents + cashiers with role badge
Period selector with presets + custom date range
Summary cards: Attendu, Collecté, Solde restant, Performance %
Collections table: one row per day, source column, écart highlighted
Inline payment recording: cash + card + mobile inputs with auto-total
Delivery detail summary shown inline per collection row
'Voir tournée complète →' link navigates to delivery page for that date
Submit collection for validation
Manager validates or rejects (with reason)
'Marquer la période comme réglée' sets isSettled=true for all collections in period
Backend: GET /cash-collections?employeeId=&startDate=&endDate= with aggregates
verification: Select an agent, see their collections for a period, record a payment, validate

backlog.description: Build /cash/reconciliations as birds-eye view across all employees. Period selector + filters (role, settled/unsettled). Table: one row per employee with period totals (tournées, attendu, collecté, solde). Solde highlighted red if negative, green if zero. Click row → navigates to /cash/collections?employee=X&period=Y. See EXPECTATIONS.md #43-45.
acceptance_criteria: Period selector with presets + custom range
Filter by role (Livreur/Caissier) and settled/unsettled status
Table: one row per employee with columns: Employé, Rôle, Tournées, Attendu, Collecté, Solde
Solde column: red if negative, green if zero, warning icon if negative
Total row at bottom
Click row → navigates to /cash/collections with employee + period pre-selected
Backend: GET /cash-collections/overview?startDate=&endDate= returns per-employee aggregates
verification: See all employees with balances, click through to detail

backlog.description: Wire /sales/transactions to real API. Replace mockProducts with useProducts(). Keep existing quick-entry UI (product cards with +/- buttons). On 'Enregistrer la vente': create deliveryRun with employee role=cashier + deliveryItems for selected products, auto-validate immediately (POS sales are instant), which triggers cash collection creation in backend. Add cashier/location selector or auto-detect from context. See EXPECTATIONS.md #25-27. [DEFERRED: clients focus on delivery operations, not POS]
acceptance_criteria: Products loaded from useProducts() (Pain Kilo 1500, Pain Moyen 250, etc.)
Cashier selector (or auto-detect current user as cashier)
Location selector (or auto-detect current boutique)
Quick-entry UI with +/- buttons preserved
Payment mode selection (Espèces/Carte/Mobile Money) preserved
'Enregistrer la vente' creates deliveryRun + items + auto-validates
Auto-validation triggers cash collection creation
Success toast with link to collection
verification: Record POS sale, validate, see collection created

backlog.description: Replace hardcoded stats on /dashboard with real API data. New backend endpoint GET /api/v1/dashboard/summary?date=. Stats cards: today's revenue (sum of sold × unitPrice from validated deliveries, in FCFA), delivery count (total + validated), collection status (settled vs pending + total collected), outstanding balance (total remainder across unsettled collections). Wire recent activity and alerts to real data. See EXPECTATIONS.md #50-52.
acceptance_criteria: Stats cards show real data: Ventes du jour, Livraisons, Collectes, Solde restant
Backend: GET /dashboard/summary returns todayRevenue, deliveryCount, collectionStatus, outstandingBalance
Quick action buttons link to correct pages
Recent activity from last N delivery validations + collection recordings
Alerts: pending collections, high return rates, outstanding balances
verification: Dashboard loads with real data from validated deliveries and collections

backlog.feature: Delivery and collection history with filters
backlog.description: Add date range, employee, and location filters to delivery list and collection list. Delivery history: filterable by date range, employee, location, status. Collection history: filterable by date range, employee, settled/unsettled. See EXPECTATIONS.md #53-54.
acceptance_criteria: Delivery list filterable by date range, employee, location, status
Collection list filterable by date range, employee, settled/unsettled
Filters persist in URL query params
Clear filters button
verification: Apply filters and verify results update

backlog.description: Outbound quantities recorded by OUT_MANAGER role, returned quantities by IN_MANAGER role. Different users handle outbound vs returns for accountability. See EXPECTATIONS.md Separation of Duties.
acceptance_criteria: OUT_MANAGER can edit entrusted quantities but not returned
IN_MANAGER can edit returned quantities but not entrusted
Role-based field locking in delivery detail view

backlog.description: For each employee: commission = sum(sold(product) × commissionPerUnit(employee, product)) for assigned products across a payroll period. Outstanding balances (remainders) adjust the final net salary: netSalary = baseSalary + totalCommission - totalRemainder. Display in employee detail or payroll view.
acceptance_criteria: Commission computed from per-product rates and sold quantities
Payroll summary: baseSalary + commission - outstanding balance = net
Visible in employee detail or dedicated payroll page

backlog.description: If delivery data is corrected after validation (admin re-opens and re-validates), the linked cash collection's expectedAmount is recalculated and all downstream balances updated. See EXPECTATIONS.md #42, #48.
acceptance_criteria: Re-validating a corrected delivery updates the linked collection's expectedAmount
Variance recalculated
Period totals updated

backlog.feature: CSV export for deliveries and collections
backlog.description: Export delivery history and collection history to CSV for audit and accounting.
acceptance_criteria: Export button on delivery list page
Export button on collection list page
CSV includes all relevant columns with FCFA amounts

backlog.feature: Fix draft delivery run location assignment
backlog.description: Auto-created draft runs use LIMIT 1 from all bakery locations instead of the employee's primary assigned location. This assigns every run to the same arbitrary location. Fix: query employee_locations WHERE isPrimary=true for each employee. Fall back to first bakery location only if no primary is set.
acceptance_criteria: Draft runs use employee's primary location from employee_locations
Falls back to first bakery location if no primary location assigned
Existing runs are not affected (only new drafts)
verification: Open deliveries for a date with no runs, verify each employee gets their correct location

backlog.description: No guard on /runs/:id/validate — a run where every quantityEntrusted=0 creates a cashCollection with expectedAmount=0, polluting collections. Add backend guard: reject validation if sum(quantityEntrusted)=0.
acceptance_criteria: POST /runs/:id/validate returns 400 if sum of quantityEntrusted across all items is 0
Error message: 'Impossible de valider une tournée sans quantité confiée'
Frontend shows toast with the error message
verification: Try to validate an all-zero run, verify it is rejected

backlog.feature: Fix variance computation on collection PATCH
backlog.description: variance is stored in DB and only recalculated when actualAmount is in the PATCH body. If someone patches only cashAmount, the stored variance goes stale. Fix: always recompute variance = (cashAmount + cardAmount + mobileAmount) - expectedAmount on any PATCH that touches payment fields.
acceptance_criteria: PATCH /cash-collections/:id with any payment field recalculates variance
variance = sum of payment fields - expectedAmount
Works even if actualAmount is not explicitly sent

backlog.description: Add sortOrder integer default 0 to employees table. Backend sorts employees by sortOrder ASC, hireDate ASC NULLS LAST. Delivery runs list also respects this order. This ensures the admin sees employees in a predictable, customizable order matching their daily workflow.
acceptance_criteria: employees table has sortOrder column (integer, default 0)
Drizzle migration generated and applied
GET /employees returns sorted by sortOrder ASC, hireDate ASC NULLS LAST
GET /delivery-runs returns runs in same employee order
Employee form allows editing sortOrder (simple number input)
verification: Verify employee list and delivery board show consistent order

backlog.description: Deactivate fires immediately on icon click with no confirmation. Wrap in ConfirmDialog like delivery item delete already uses. Include employee name in the confirmation message.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking deactivate icon shows ConfirmDialog
Dialog shows employee name and warns about consequences
Cancel dismisses, Confirm deactivates
Same pattern for reactivate (optional)

backlog.description: isDirty is computed but never surfaced. Clicking another employee row while edits are pending discards all changes silently. Show ConfirmDialog when switching runs while isDirty: 'Vous avez des modifications non enregistrées. Continuer sans sauvegarder ?'
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a different employee row when isDirty shows confirmation dialog
User can choose to discard or cancel
If user confirms, switches to new run and resets local state
If user cancels, stays on current run
verification: Edit quantities, click another employee, verify dialog appears

backlog.description: Backend has POST /:id/validate and /:id/reject endpoints but frontend has no buttons. When status=submitted, show Valider (green) and Rejeter (red, with reason input) buttons in the expanded PaymentForm area.
acceptance_criteria: Submitted collections show Valider and Rejeter buttons
Valider calls POST /:id/validate and refreshes
Rejeter shows reason input, calls POST /:id/reject with reason
Validated collections show read-only view
Rejected collections show rejection reason badge

backlog.feature: PaymentForm inline accordion + collections URL params
backlog.description: Two fixes: (1) PaymentForm renders below entire table instead of inline with the row — move it into a <tr> after the clicked row. (2) Collections page doesn't read URL search params from reconciliations drill-down — initialize selectedEmployeeId and period from useSearchParams().
acceptance_criteria: PaymentForm expands as a table row immediately below the clicked collection
Navigating from reconciliations pre-fills employee and date range
Custom period mode activated when startDate/endDate params present
verification: Click Saisir on middle row, verify form appears inline
Navigate from reconciliations, verify employee pre-selected

backlog.feature: Collections backend — batch queries and SQL filters
backlog.description: Three performance fixes: (1) GET /cash-collections does N+1 per row for employee/run lookup — batch fetch. (2) All filters (startDate, endDate, employeeId, etc.) applied in JS after full table scan — push to SQL WHERE. (3) Overview endpoint does N+1 per employee — use SQL GROUP BY. (4) Settle endpoint does sequential UPDATE per row — batch with WHERE id IN.
acceptance_criteria: GET /cash-collections uses batch employee/run lookup (like deliveries list)
Date range and employeeId filters pushed to Drizzle WHERE clauses
GET /overview uses SQL aggregation or single-pass computation
POST /settle uses single UPDATE ... WHERE id IN (...)
No N+1 queries in collections routes
verification: Load collections page, verify response time improved

backlog.description: Two fixes: (1) GET /runs/:id single run detail does Promise.all with one DB query per item for product names — batch fetch product IDs. (2) GET /employees does N+1 for locations per employee — batch fetch with inArray.
acceptance_criteria: GET /runs/:id fetches all products in one query
GET /employees fetches all locations in one query using inArray
No N+1 patterns remain in deliveries or employees routes

backlog.feature: Delivery page — Aujourd'hui button, period auto-pick, misc
backlog.description: Bundle of small UX fixes: (1) Add Aujourd'hui button between date arrows. (2) +Période auto-picks next unused period instead of hardcoding Après-midi. (3) Rename Réinitialiser to Remettre à zéro with RotateCcw icon, only show when values > 0. (4) Summary stats in detail panel get visual cards instead of plain text.
acceptance_criteria: Aujourd'hui button appears, jumps to current date
+Période picks next unused period for that product
Réinitialiser renamed with distinct icon, hidden when already zero
Detail panel summary uses styled cards

backlog.feature: Employee page — form improvements and table cleanup
backlog.description: Bundle: (1) Replace commissionRate column with product assignment count badge. (2) Add select all/deselect all toggle in product assignment panel. (3) Widen dialog to max-w-2xl, increase product list max-h-64. (4) Hide email/phone icons when values empty. (5) Fix product assignment race condition (clear on 0 assignments).
acceptance_criteria: Table shows '3 produits' badge instead of commission %
Product panel has Tout sélectionner toggle
Dialog wider, product list taller
No dangling icons for empty email/phone
Opening edit for employee with 0 assignments shows empty product list

backlog.feature: Cross-cutting — error boundary, sidebar active state, mobile padding
backlog.description: Bundle: (1) Add app/(app)/error.tsx catch-all error boundary. (2) Sidebar uses usePathname() to highlight current page. (3) Main content gets p-4 on mobile (currently 0). (4) Remove dead employeeOptions code in collections page. (5) Fix variance color semantics in collections (green=0, red=negative, amber=positive).
acceptance_criteria: Error boundary catches unhandled exceptions with retry button
Current sidebar item is visually highlighted
Content has padding on mobile screens
No dead code warnings
Variance colors match reconciliations page semantics

backlog.description: Add isArchived flag to cashCollections. Settled periods can be archived to hide from default view. Backend: POST /:id/archive endpoint to toggle isArchived. Frontend: Archive button for settled collections, default filter hides archived, 'Voir les archivés' toggle to show archived rows with muted styling. Archived data is never deleted, just hidden by default.
acceptance_criteria: cashCollections.isArchived boolean field in schema
Drizzle migration generated and applied
POST /cash-collections/:id/archive toggles isArchived
GET /cash-collections accepts includeArchived query param (default false)
Collections UI: Archive button for settled collections
Default view hides archived collections
Toggle 'Voir les archivés' shows archived rows (muted styling)
